title: silene parnassica subsp. hayekiana
intro: Silene parnassica subsp. hayekiana, also known as Hayek's catchfly, is a plant species within the carnation family. It is used in traditional medicine for its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing inflammation and soothing digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Silene parnassica subsp. hayekiana can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are typically used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory ailments.
